description: Dependencies and devDependencies use caret ranges instead of exact pins; a committed package-lock.json fully mitigates this in practice
fp_reason: Caret ranges are standard npm convention and the repo commits package-lock.json, so resolved versions are deterministic in CI/installs
rule_gap: SEC-unpinned-semver should check for an absent lockfile before flagging, not just scan manifest range operators

evidence: File body contains no '<example>' or '## Example' section and no sample Agent(...) invocation or sample output
description: Agent body has zero example/invocation blocks
suggested_fix: Add an example showing Agent({subagent_type: "auditor", prompt: ...}) and a sample findings report

evidence: Body ends at 'Be concise.' with no structural template for the findings report
description: No explicit output-format section defining the structure of the security findings report
suggested_fix: Add an '## Output Format' section specifying file path, line, severity, and remediation columns

description: 21 e2e fixture agents lack a formatted example/invocation block; each instead describes an input config to expected-tool-set mapping in prose
suggested_fix: Not necessary for e2e scaffolding; leave as-is

description: Generic rubric check for missing 'name' frontmatter does not apply — this ecosystem derives the agent type name from the filename, not a frontmatter field, and this holds across all 22 scored files
fp_reason: README.md:166,209 documents that '.pi/agents/<name>.md' filename is the agent type name; 'name' is not a recognized frontmatter field in this ecosystem, so registration never breaks
rule_gap: NLPM's tier-aware overlay system has no 'pi' custom-agent overlay; the Claude-Code-style 'name: required' check needs an ecosystem exception for filename-derived agent names

description: Generic rubric check 'model not declared' misfires on these 21 e2e fixtures, which intentionally omit model to test tool/extension scoping, not model behavior
fp_reason: README.md:218 documents model as optional, defaulting to inherit-parent by design; these fixtures test tools/extensions, not model selection
rule_gap: R10 should be scoped to agents whose stated purpose involves model-sensitive behavior, or excluded for ecosystems with a documented inherit-by-default model policy
